acquisition method: The MS data is acquired in the data-dependent mode with the top-speed option.
alkylation reagent: alkylated in 55 mM iodoacetamide (Sigma Aldrich, Germany)
cleavage agent: digested with trypsin, as previously described.
collision energy: one of three collision energy settings (26%, 28% or 30%) was selected for fragmentation
enrichment method: we enriched for the ubiquitinated complex by affinity chromatography using the His-tag on ubiquitin
fragmentation method: isolated and fragmented using Higher-energy collisional dissociation (HCD).
instrument: performed using an Orbitrap Fusion™ Lumos™ Tribrid™ mass spectrometer.
ionization type: Eluted peptides were ionized by an EASY-Spray source (Thermo Scientific).
labeling: peptides were re-suspended with the sample loading buffer (0.1% v/v formic acid (FA), 1.6% v/v ACN) to a concentration of ~ 0.3 μg μl-1, and 3.5 μl was injected for LC-MS/MS acquisition.
reduction reagent: reduced in 10 mM dithiothreitol (Sigma Aldrich, Germany)
reduction concentration: reduced in 10 mM dithiothreitol (Sigma Aldrich, Germany)
